codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers expected behavior: detects contradictory memories using cosine distance (0.15-0.40 range) with content similarity fallback, applies all specified resolution strategies (higher_confidence_wins, newer_wins, operator_overrides, keep_original, keep_new, merge, manual), and logs all conflict resolution decisions via tracing::info! with audit trail via ConflictResolutionLogEntry. 19 tests pass covering conflict detection, resolution strategies, source priority ordering, logging, and edge cases.

issues.description: The `is_contradiction` function uses simple keyword matching (e.g., 'always' vs 'never') which could produce false positives. For example, 'always running tests' and 'never skip tests' would be flagged as contradictory when they are semantically aligned. This is a reasonable heuristic for MVP but could be improved with better NLP.

The `OutdatedSupersession` conflict type uses hardcoded `age_diff > 30` days threshold. This could cause test flakiness if memory timestamps are close. Consider making this configurable via ConflictResolutionConfig.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No convention gaps identified. The worker followed AGENTS.md conventions: used async_trait where needed, proper error handling patterns, tracing for logging, and unit tests in #[cfg(test)] modules.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All 19 tests are in #[cfg(test)] mod tests, tracing::info! used for logging as specified in AGENTS.md

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed Rust skill procedure correctly. No deviations from skill feedback.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.shows followedProcedure: true with no deviations

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new services were started. The worker only used standard cargo commands (build, test, clippy) which are already documented in .factory/services.yaml.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Verification commands run were cargo build --workspace and cargo test -p swell-memory -- conflict

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new knowledge gaps discovered. The worker successfully implemented the feature by understanding the existing swell-memory crate structure and MemoryEntry/MemoryBlock types from swell-core.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker read existing files (lib.rs, semantic.rs, pattern_learning.rs) and grepped for MemoryEntry, MemoryBlock, and confidence patterns to understand the codebase before implementing

summary: mem-conflict-resolution feature passes review. Implementation is complete with ConflictDetector for detecting conflicts via cosine distance (0.15-0.40) or content similarity fallback, ConflictResolver with 7 resolution strategies (higher_confidence_wins default, newer_wins, operator_overrides, keep_original, keep_new, merge, manual), MemorySource priority system (operator feedback > task success > task failure > learned patterns), and ConflictResolutionLogEntry for audit trail. All 19 tests pass. Two minor non-blocking issues noted: simple keyword-based contradiction detection could have false positives, and hardcoded 30-day age threshold could cause test flakiness.
